currency.name: Euro
currency.budgetNote: Recommended: 30 EUR (Montenegro uses the Euro even though not in the EU)

mapQuestions.question: Which continent is Montenegro on?
mapQuestions.answer: Europe, in the Balkans on the Adriatic coast. Tiny: about half the size of Wales 🌍

mapQuestions.question: What is the capital of Montenegro?
mapQuestions.answer: Podgorica, in the south. Small (around 175,000 people) and not very visited. Most travellers head straight for the coast or the mountains.

mapQuestions.question: What does "Montenegro" mean?
mapQuestions.answer: Italian/Venetian for "black mountain", named after Mount Lovćen, which used to look black from a distance because of its dense forests. The country's own name for itself is "Crna Gora", which means the same thing.

mapQuestions.question: What language does Montenegro speak?
mapQuestions.answer: Montenegrin (very similar to Serbian, Croatian and Bosnian, all mutually intelligible). Most younger Montenegrins also speak good English.

mapQuestions.question: When did Montenegro become its own country?
mapQuestions.answer: 2006, when it peacefully split from a brief union with Serbia. Montenegro is one of the youngest countries in Europe.

foods.name: Buzara
foods.description: Mussels or prawns simmered in white wine, garlic, parsley and a touch of tomato. The Bay of Kotor speciality, served with fresh bread.

foods.name: Njeguški pršut
foods.description: Air-cured smoked ham from the village of Njeguši, hung in the mountain wind for months. Served sliced thin with cheese and bread. The local prosciutto.

foods.name: Kajmak
foods.description: A thick, fresh, salty mountain dairy product (somewhere between butter and clotted cream). Spread on bread or melted over grilled meat.

foods.name: Burek
foods.description: Flaky pastry filled with cheese, meat or spinach. The Balkan everyday snack, eaten with yogurt for breakfast.

foods.name: Skadar Lake carp
foods.description: Freshwater carp from Lake Skadar (Europe's biggest lake by surface area), often grilled and served with potato.

foods.name: Mountain honey
foods.description: Wild honey from the Montenegrin mountains, especially chestnut honey, dark and full-flavoured.

scavengerItems.label: The Bay of Kotor, a fjord-like Adriatic inlet ringed by mountains
The walled medieval old town of Kotor (UNESCO World Heritage)
Our Lady of the Rocks, a tiny artificial island church in the Bay of Kotor
Mount Lovćen with the Njegoš Mausoleum at the top (the highest mausoleum in the world)
Lake Skadar, Europe's biggest lake by surface area, half-Montenegro half-Albania
A clear Adriatic beach (Sveti Stefan, Budva, Petrovac)
The "Tara Canyon", the deepest river canyon in Europe (1,300 m deep)
A small boat in the calm fjord-like waters of Kotor
An ox or donkey on a winding mountain road in Durmitor
A monastery clinging to a cliff (Ostrog Monastery, built into a rockface)
Wildflowers in the alpine meadows of Durmitor in spring
A golden eagle soaring above the Durmitor peaks

geoMatches.description: A medieval walled town at the head of the Bay of Kotor (a deep fjord-like inlet). Cobbled lanes, Venetian-era palaces, cats everywhere.
A spectacular deep Adriatic inlet that looks like a Norwegian fjord, ringed by 1,700 m mountains. UNESCO World Heritage.
The biggest coastal resort. Walled old town on the sea, sandy/pebble beaches, busy nightlife. Montenegro's "Riviera".
A tiny ex-fishing-village island connected to the mainland by a narrow strand. Now a luxury hotel. Photographed from every angle.
A national park in the north with the Tara Canyon (the deepest river canyon in Europe), 18 glacial lakes, and rugged hiking.
A mountain just inland of Kotor, with the marble Njegoš Mausoleum on top (built into the peak itself). Reached by 25 hairpin bends from Kotor.

senses.smell: Pine and rosemary on Kotor cliffs, fresh herring grilled at a Bay restaurant, mountain herbs in the Durmitor meadows, salt and seaweed on a Budva beach, wood-fired bread from a village oven…
senses.hear: "Zdravo!" greetings, the slap of Adriatic waves on Kotor stones, cathedral bells across the Bay, mountain rivers in Tara Canyon, the wind across Lovćen ridges…
senses.taste: Salty buzara mussels in white-wine sauce, sharp smoked Njeguški ham, mountain honey with fresh kajmak, lake-carp grilled with lemon, sweet Montenegrin figs in summer…
senses.feel: Warm Adriatic water in the Bay, the cold of a glacier lake in Durmitor, hot Kotor stones underfoot, the steep climb of the Kotor city walls, the cool inside of Ostrog Monastery rock…
senses.see: What is the most incredible thing right in front of you right now…

stories.question: Is the Bay of Kotor a fjord?
stories.body: Technically no, but it sure looks like one. The Bay of Kotor is a "ria", a deep river valley flooded by rising sea level (rather than a true fjord carved by glaciers, like Norway's). Either way, it is a spectacular long Adriatic inlet that twists inland for 28 km, ringed by 1,700 m mountains rising almost vertically from the water's edge. UNESCO put the whole Bay on the World Heritage list in 1979. The walled medieval city of Kotor sits at the head of the inlet, with city walls running 1,200 m UP the mountain behind it. The bay used to belong to the Venetian Empire, and you can see Venetian palaces all along the coast.

stories.question: How do you build a monastery INTO a cliff?
stories.body: Ostrog Monastery, in the rocky interior of Montenegro, was built in the 1600s directly into a vertical white cliff face about 900 m up. The lower monastery building is normal stone construction; the upper monastery is two churches and a small cell, all built into a natural cave carved into the rock. The founder, Saint Basil of Ostrog, lived in the cave for 15 years and is buried inside. Pilgrims from all over the Balkans walk barefoot up the steep road to visit the upper monastery, especially on saint days. Christians, Muslims and Roma all consider Ostrog a sacred place.

stories.question: How deep is the Tara Canyon?
stories.body: The Tara River canyon in northern Montenegro is the DEEPEST river canyon in Europe: 1,300 metres from rim to river. (For comparison, the Grand Canyon in the USA is about 1,800 m at its deepest.) The canyon is 78 km long and carved into bright white limestone, with thick forests on the slopes. The Tara River that made it is one of the cleanest rivers in Europe, with water so pure you can sometimes drink straight from it. White-water rafting through the canyon (4-day trips through Durmitor National Park) is one of Montenegro's biggest adventure draws.
stories.thinkingQuestion: Europe's deepest river canyon is in one of its smallest countries. What other small countries hide oversized natural features?

stories.question: What is on top of Mount Lovćen?
stories.body: Mount Lovćen (1,749 m) rises just inland of Kotor and was the symbol of free Montenegro for centuries (when the Ottomans controlled most of the Balkans, the mountains around Lovćen were too hard to conquer). On the summit sits the Njegoš Mausoleum, a marble tomb of the great 19th-century Montenegrin prince-bishop-poet Petar II Petrović-Njegoš. It is the highest mausoleum in the world. To reach it you climb 461 steps inside the mountain through a tunnel. At the top, you stand on a viewing platform with one of the most spectacular panoramas in Europe: the Bay of Kotor below, the Albanian Alps in the distance, half of Montenegro spread out around you.

stories.question: Why is Montenegro one of the youngest countries in Europe?
stories.body: Montenegro spent centuries as a small, fierce mountain kingdom that the Ottoman Empire never fully conquered (its terrain made invasion incredibly difficult). After World War I, Montenegro joined the new Kingdom of Yugoslavia. After Yugoslavia broke up in the 1990s, Montenegro stayed in a small federation with Serbia. In 2006, Montenegrins voted in a referendum to peacefully split from Serbia and become fully independent. The split was settled with paperwork, not war. Montenegro joined NATO in 2017 and is currently a candidate for EU membership. It is one of the youngest countries in Europe.

convoQuestions.question: Best food in Montenegro so far?
Bay of Kotor, mountains, canyon, beach, which has felt the most "Montenegro"?
How does the Bay of Kotor compare to other coastal places we have seen?
Did you climb the Kotor city walls? How far up did you get?
Montenegro is one of Europe's smallest and newest countries. How does that feel as you travel through it?

wordSearch.hint: Montenegrin foods, places and things to spot.

animals.name: Brown bear
animals.description: Lives in the deep forests of Durmitor and Biogradska Gora national parks.

animals.name: Red deer
animals.description: In the alpine forests of northern Montenegro, especially Biogradska Gora.

animals.name: Grey wolf
animals.description: Wolves have re-established in the Durmitor and Komovi mountain ranges.

animals.name: Kotor cat
animals.description: Kotor's old town is famously full of cats, lounging on every walls, doorstep and Venetian palace. There is even a cat museum.

animals.name: Golden eagle
animals.description: Nests in the high crags of Durmitor National Park. About 50 pairs in the country.

animals.name: Horn-nosed viper
animals.description: A venomous viper found in the limestone hills of Montenegro. Shy, gives warning, almost never bites.

animals.name: Otter
animals.description: Returning to the clean waters of the Tara River and Lake Skadar.
